conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So you've been feeling okay about the commute then? What's been taking up most of your time lately?
You know, just the usual bar work and trying to get my own place in order. I mean, it's been busy.
That sounds like a lot on your plate. How have you been sleeping lately?
Honestly, not great. I've been having trouble falling asleep at night because I'm worried about some things.
It sounds like sleep is a bit of an issue for you right now. Can you tell me more about what's got you worried?
Yeah, just work stuff mostly. The guilt trips I make myself take when things don't go as planned.
Guilt can be a tough one to deal with. Have you noticed this affecting your daily routine at all?
Actually, yeah. I've been putting off some tasks that need doing and then getting stressed about it later.
So it's like you're procrastinating because of feelings of guilt? How has work been handling that?
They haven't said anything yet, but I can tell they're not pleased. It's making me anxious about the future.
I see. You mentioned anxiety earlier – is that something that's been a constant companion for you lately?
Yeah, it comes and goes. But when I'm lying awake at night thinking about work, it's hard to shake off.
It sounds like sleep deprivation might be contributing to some of those feelings of guilt. Have you tried anything to help with sleep?
Not really – I just try to tell myself it's fine and get up earlier the next day instead.
Okay, and what about your coworkers or friends – how have they been handling all this stress?
They're being supportive, but it's hard for me to open up sometimes. I don't want to be a downer.
It takes a lot of courage to share your struggles with others. Have you considered talking to someone about what's going on?
Yeah, maybe soon. My sister suggested therapy a while back, but I haven't gotten around to it yet.
That's great that she cares about you – maybe we can explore some coping strategies for you in the meantime? How do you usually deal with stress when it gets bad?
Um, honestly? I don't know. Sometimes I just try to push through and hope things get better on their own.
Okay, well let's work on building some new skills together. What are your goals for our time here today?
Just trying to figure out how to deal with this guilt and get a handle on my sleep again – maybe learn some relaxation techniques too.
We can definitely work on those things. Is there anything specific that's been keeping you up at night lately?
Yeah, just worrying about making mistakes and not meeting expectations.
Okay, we'll start by exploring those worries further. Are there any times when you have felt like you're really accomplishing something in your daily routine?
Actually, yeah – when I'm bartending and people are having a good time, that usually makes me feel better about things.
Ah, it sounds like some positive interactions with others can help boost your mood. How often do you get to experience those moments?
When I'm working, pretty regularly. But sometimes I feel guilty for not being able to attend events or hang out more because of work demands.
That makes sense. It's like you're caught between two worlds – the social world and your job. Have you noticed any patterns in how this guilt affects your daily routine?
Yeah, it usually hits after I've missed a deadline or something. Then I beat myself up over it and get anxious.
Okay, so there's a clear trigger for those feelings of guilt – deadlines. How have you been handling those since they're not going away anytime soon?
Honestly? I just put them off until the last minute and then stress about meeting them.
Okay, let's work on building some coping skills around those deadlines. What do you think would make a difference for you if you were doing better with time management?
Maybe getting more organized – setting aside dedicated blocks of time to focus on tasks without distractions.
That sounds like a great place to start. How does that sound?
